cards.title: Anthropic's $1.5B Copyright Settlement Approved
cards.body: Anthropic has secured final approval for a landmark $1.5B copyright settlement. However, the agreement doesn't resolve broader questions about using copyrighted works to train AI models.

cards.title: Trump's AI Czar Role Becomes Revolving Door
cards.body: David Sacks has resigned from his position as director of the Center for AI Standards and Innovation. The role has struggled with retention since its establishment.

cards.title: Google Develops New Chip for Gemini Efficiency
cards.body: Alphabet is working on a custom chip designed to make Gemini models run significantly more efficiently. The project aims to reduce computational overhead for the company's AI products.

cards.title: AI Protocol Gets Easier to Use
cards.body: A key AI protocol is adopting a looser, stateless approach to session IDs on the server side. This change mirrors how most standard websites currently operate.

cards.title: X Launches Rebuilt Android App Globally
cards.body: X has completed a year-long rebuild of its Android application, now available worldwide. The new version represents a significant technical overhaul of the platform.

cards.title: OpenAI Concerned About Open-Weight AI Models
cards.body: OpenAI expresses concern over open-weight LLMs, particularly Chinese-made alternatives, raising questions about their business impact. The debate highlights tensions between open-source development and commercial AI strategies.

cards.title: Adobe's New AI Photo Critique Feature Launches
cards.body: Adobe's Project Indigo now removes all kinds of backgrounds from photos captured through the app. The feature leverages AI to streamline photo editing workflows.

cards.title: YouTube Updates AI Content Monetization Policies
cards.body: YouTube has clarified its monetization policies to better define AI-generated and low-quality content restrictions. Videos meeting these criteria will no longer be eligible for ad revenue.

cards.title: Jensen Huang Secures Major Japan Tech Deals
cards.body: NVIDIA's CEO Jensen Huang concluded his Japan visit with deals spanning the nation's entire tech ecosystem. The agreements strengthen NVIDIA's presence in the Asian market.

cards.title: Apple's Lawsuit May Impact OpenAI's IPO
cards.body: Apple has filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming misconduct involving over 400 former Apple employees. The legal action could complicate OpenAI's planned hardware expansion and public offering.

cards.title: Christopher Nolan Calls AI a Trojan Horse
cards.body: Director Christopher Nolan warned that AI represents an obvious threat, describing it as a Trojan horse. His comments reflect growing concerns about AI's implications in creative industries.

cards.title: Current AI Builds Decentralized World Wide Web
cards.body: Nonprofit Current AI is developing an open-source AI infrastructure designed to serve all cultures globally. The project has made significant progress in cross-device compatibility and AI chat.

cards.title: Moonshot AI Releases New Kimi Model Version
cards.body: Chinese company Moonshot AI unveiled a new version of its Kimi model this week, triggering global discussion. The release raises questions about international AI competition and dominance.

cards.title: VC Predicts AI Wealth Redistribution Coming
cards.body: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer predicts the historic wealth generated by AI will be redistributed across society. He suggests this redistribution could occur either voluntarily or involuntarily.

cards.title: Vertu's $6,880 AI Agent Phone Reviewed
cards.body: Luxury brand Vertu is offering executives an AI agent-powered foldable phone at a $6,880 price point. Real-world testing reveals performance, battery life, and security considerations.

cards.title: Databricks Reaches $188B Valuation Milestone
cards.body: Databricks has transformed into a leading AI company, achieving a $188B valuation in its latest round. The company published research demonstrating cost savings from open-weight AI models.

cards.title: Zoom Meeting Transcription Raises Privacy Concerns
cards.body: A new Zoom feature enables users to opt out of meeting recordings and transcriptions. The capability reflects growing concerns about universal meeting capture and data usage.

cards.title: Agility Robotics Opens Fremont Training Center
cards.body: Agility Robotics is establishing a new training facility for its Digit robots in Fremont, California. The move represents expansion of the company's robotics operations in Tesla's backyard.

cards.title: AI Chip Demand Disrupts India's Smartphone Market
cards.body: India's smartphone slowdown reflects how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics globally. The shift affects pricing, demand patterns, and corporate strategy across the region.

cards.title: Apple Files Trade Secrets Lawsuit Against OpenAI
cards.body: Apple alleges OpenAI engaged in misconduct involving confidential information and over 400 former Apple employees. The lawsuit threatens to disrupt OpenAI's hardware plans and anticipated IPO.

cards.title: Anthropic's $1.5B copyright settlement approved
cards.body: The final approval settles one case, but doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models. This landmark agreement represents a major step in addressing copyright concerns in the AI industry.

cards.title: Google develops new efficient Gemini AI chip
cards.body: Alphabet is working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently. The hardware advancement could significantly improve performance and reduce computational costs.

cards.title: OpenAI fears open-weight model competition
cards.body: OpenAI is reportedly concerned about Chinese-made open-weight LLMs, raising questions about whether the US should impose restrictions. The tension reveals challenges in turning AI into a sustainable business model.

cards.title: Adobe's AI photo critique feature launches
cards.body: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos and provide AI-powered critique. The feature brings advanced AI capabilities directly to mobile photography.

cards.title: YouTube tightens AI slop monetization rules
cards.body: YouTube updated its monetization policies to clearly define AI-generated and low-quality videos that can't earn ad revenue. The move aims to maintain content quality and creator trust on the platform.

cards.title: Apple lawsuit threatens OpenAI hardware plans
cards.body: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI alleging misconduct and claiming over 400 former Apple employees work there. The legal action could significantly disrupt OpenAI's IPO and hardware ambitions.

cards.title: Current AI builds nonprofit open-source alternative
cards.body: Current AI, a nonprofit, is building accessible AI that leaves no culture behind with progress across devices and chat. The initiative aims to democratize AI technology globally without commercial restrictions.

cards.title: Moonshot AI's Kimi raises global security concerns
cards.body: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model, prompting concerns about competitive AI development. The advancement highlights geopolitical tensions in the global AI race.

cards.title: Databricks valued at $188B as AI favorite
cards.body: Databricks has positioned itself as an AI company and published research on cost savings of open-weight models for coding. The valuation reflects growing investor confidence in AI infrastructure platforms.

cards.title: AI hiring bias research reveals critical flaws
cards.body: Research shows LLMs are more likely than humans to form biases when screening resumes, developing their own prejudices beyond training data. The finding raises serious concerns about AI-driven recruitment fairness.

cards.title: OpenAI builds GPT-Red AI security model
cards.body: OpenAI created GPT-Red, an LLM super-hacker that acts as a sparring partner to boost model defenses against cyberattacks. The security tool helped make GPT-5.6 significantly more resilient.

cards.title: AI coding harness offers context-rich alternative
cards.body: Developers are exploring AI coding tools that provide deeper contextual understanding beyond traditional grep functionality. These advanced harnesses could significantly improve code quality and development efficiency.

cards.title: AI faces challenges with prior authorization
cards.body: Healthcare AI systems designed to streamline prior authorization face questions about whether they improve or worsen the process. The technology's real-world impact on patient care remains uncertain.
